title: Learning Rate and Epochs in SFT
concept: SFT uses smaller learning rates and fewer epochs (usually 1-3) than pretraining, as the goal is to align style, not learn new language facts.

exercises.prompt: What is the main goal of Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT)?

options.text: To teach the model how to read and write from scratch.
To adjust the style and behavior of an already smart model.
To make the model run faster on mobile phones.

exercises.explain: SFT is about fine-tuning the style and behavior of a model that is already smart, not teaching it raw facts from scratch.

cards.title: Gentle Adjustments
cards.body: When we fine-tune an AI, we don't want to rebuild its brain from scratch. We just want to teach it some manners! How do we make sure we don't break what it already knows?

cards.title: SFT Settings
cards.body: During Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT), we use a tiny learning rate (making small adjustments) and very few epochs (usually 1 to 3 passes over the data). This prevents the model from forgetting the vast language knowledge it already has.

cards.title: The Chef's Masterclass
cards.body: Imagine you are a trained chef learning how to plate dishes at a new, fancy restaurant. You don't need to go back to culinary school for 4 years (pretraining). You just need a quick, gentle masterclass of 1 or 2 sessions (epochs) to learn the new style.

cards.title: Polishing Customer Service
cards.body: If we fine-tune a model to write helpful customer service emails, we only show it the email database 2 times (2 epochs) with a very small learning step. This keeps the model smart while teaching it to sound polite and professional.

cards.title: More is Not Better
cards.body: People often think that more training epochs always make a model better. But in SFT, training for too long (overfitting) actually damages the model, making it lose its general knowledge and repeat itself!

cards.title: Pretraining vs. SFT
cards.body: See how the settings compare between the initial heavy training phase and the fine-tuning phase.

right.text: Tiny learning rate, 1-3 epochs. Goal: Align style and behavior.

left.text: High learning rate, thousands of epochs. Goal: Learn language basics.

exercises.prompt: Why does SFT use much smaller learning rates and fewer epochs than pretraining?

options.text: To protect existing knowledge and align style instead of rewriting the brain.
Because training computers is expensive and we want to save electricity.
To make the model completely forget everything it learned before.
Because SFT datasets are always billions of pages long.

exercises.explain: SFT is about style alignment, not teaching basic language. High learning rates would destroy the pre-trained knowledge.

exercises.prompt: True or False: SFT typically requires hundreds of epochs to get the best results.
exercises.explain: SFT only needs 1 to 3 epochs because it is adapting style, not learning a language from scratch.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to complete the rule of thumb for Supervised Fine-Tuning.
exercises.sentence: SFT requires a small learning rate and very few epochs to avoid ruining the model.
exercises.explain: Since the model already knows how to speak, we only need to pass over the fine-tuning data 1 to 3 times (epochs) to change its style.

exercises.prompt: Two AI developers are setting up SFT. AI Model A is set to a low learning rate and 2 epochs. AI Model B is set to a high learning rate and 50 epochs. Which model is set up correctly?

options.text: AI Model B, because bigger numbers mean better performance.
AI Model A, because it uses a low learning rate and only 2 epochs.
Both models will perform exactly the same.

exercises.explain: AI Model B has the typical hallmarks of over-tuning (high epochs and high learning rate for SFT), which often ruins the model's general abilities. AI Model A uses correct SFT settings.

exercises.prompt: You are ready to train your model using Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT). Which training settings should you choose?

options.text: High learning rate and 50+ epochs, to force the model to memorize new facts.
Low learning rate and 1 to 3 epochs, to gently align style without erasing knowledge.
Zero epochs, because the model does not change at all during SFT.
High learning rate and 1 epoch, to quickly scramble the model's brain.

exercises.explain: SFT is about polishing and adapting a model's style, not teaching it a whole new language. Because of this, it uses a tiny learning rate to avoid erasing old knowledge, and only needs 1 to 3 epochs.
